rule: raw:m5-bnf-text-drift-fix-omega-cycle (Tier-2 doc-amendment from 2026-04-26 m5 i1+i2 code-emission ω-cycle l4 backlog: BNF text missing two productions that corpus already exercised and parser already accepts — text-only doc fix, no code change).
subject: Augment spec/m5_ordinal_bnf.txt with the two grammar productions surfaced as gaps during the i1+i2 code-emission round (E1 trailing-nat summand, E2 bare-tower exponent). BNF grows from 30 lines / sha256 3b9a77... to 37 lines / sha256 4edce6... — adds `('+' nat)?` to sum, `term` alternative to exp, two side-conditions SC5+SC6, three concrete examples EX1..EX3 anchored to corpus rows 9/32/37. NO parser code change, NO corpus change — text-doc-only.
purpose: Close the spec-vs-impl documentation drift recorded in design/meta_engine/2026-04-26_m5_i1i2_code_emission_omega_cycle.json axis l4 (Tier-2 backlog). After this lands, BNF text is byte-derivable on every spec/m5_ordinal_corpus.tsv input, parser tool/m5_ordinal.py is provably under-spec rather than over-accepting. Falsifier closure: future readers can ω+1 / ω^ω^ω from BNF alone without consulting parser source.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'spec-augment ω-cycle for m5 BNF text drift fix — augment the two missing productions, preserve 5-line core, add side-conditions, ≤40 lines, verify against corpus, update sha256 in two upstream witnesses, re-run test, write witness'.
method: spec-text augment, single-pass edit with regression-gate. Procedure: (1) read existing 30-line BNF, (2) extend `sum` and `exp` productions in-place, (3) append SC5+SC6 side-conditions explaining trailing-nat and bare-tower right-assoc semantics, (4) append EX1..EX3 corpus-anchored example block, (5) verify ≤40 lines, (6) recompute sha256, (7) update sha256 in upstream spec-cycle witness `axes_surfaced[k1].sha256` + `first_impl_target.spec_artifacts_committed_this_round[0]` + code-emission cycle `spec_oracle_sha256[spec/m5_ordinal_bnf.txt]`, (8) re-run python3 tool/m5_ordinal_test.py — must remain `__M5_ORDINAL__ PASS corpus=50 fuzzer=10` exit 0 (parser unchanged, BNF augment is doc-only). omega_stop = 4 axes saturated, BNF ≤40 lines, test green, sha256 propagated.
ordinal_self_stamp_basis: Inherits parent code-emission cycle Γ_0+1. Doc-amendment is a sibling artifact-emission within the same predicative plane — text correction of an existing artifact, not a new deepening step. Stamp held flat at Γ_0+1.

axes_surfaced.name: trailing_nat_summand_production
axes_surfaced.summary: Extended `sum ::= term ('+' term)*` to `sum ::= term ('+' term)* ('+' nat)?`. Captures corpus inputs like `ω+1`, `ω+5`, `ω^2+ω·2`-style tails where the final summand is bare nat (semantically ω^0·n term collapsed to its coefficient). Added SC5 side-condition: optional final '+ nat' denotes ω^0·n, may appear at most once and only in tail position, preserves SC1 strict-descent because ω^0 is strictly less than any β_prev derivable from a 'term' production.
axes_surfaced.falsifier: BNF must derive corpus row 9 `ω+1` and row 11 `ω+1000` from `sum` non-terminal with the new production. If derivation requires the old `term` form for the trailing nat, augment is incomplete.

axes_surfaced.name: bare_tower_exponent_production
axes_surfaced.summary: Extended `exp ::= '0' | nat | '(' ordinal ')'` to `exp ::= '0' | nat | term | '(' ordinal ')'`. Captures corpus inputs like `ω^ω`, `ω^ω^ω`, `ω^ω^ω^ω` where exponent is a bare term (no parens) that itself may carry `^exp` recursively. Added SC6 side-condition: bare-term exponent is right-associative — `ω^ω^ω` parses as `ω^(ω^ω)`, NOT `(ω^ω)^ω`. Operator '^' associates right when exponent is itself a 'term'.
axes_surfaced.falsifier: BNF must derive corpus row 32 `ω^ω^ω` and row 37 `ω^ω^ω^ω` from `exp` non-terminal with the new production. Right-associativity must give `ω^(ω^ω) < ε_0` (matches corpus row 32 LT result) — left-associativity would give `(ω^ω)^ω = ω^(ω·ω) = ω^ω^2 < ε_0` which is also LT but is the WRONG ordinal — only right-assoc matches the parser semantics on tool/m5_ordinal.py.

axes_surfaced.name: corpus_anchored_example_block
axes_surfaced.summary: Appended EX1..EX3 concrete-example block citing three corpus rows that exercised the drift: EX1 = row 9 `ω+1` (trailing-nat via SC5, parses [(ω^1,1),(ω^0,1)]); EX2 = row 32 `ω^ω^ω` (bare-tower via SC6, parses ω^(ω^ω) right-assoc); EX3 = row 37 `ω^ω^ω^ω` (deep bare-tower via SC6, parses ω^(ω^(ω^ω))). Each example pin-points the exact SC and expected parse-tree shape, making BNF self-contained for future readers.
axes_surfaced.falsifier: each EX must be a literal substring of spec/m5_ordinal_corpus.tsv column 'a' or 'b' for the cited row index. Cross-check: row 9 `ω+1`, row 32 `ω^ω^ω`, row 37 `ω^ω^ω^ω` — all confirmed present in corpus.

axes_surfaced.name: test_run_post_augment_green
axes_surfaced.summary: Re-ran `python3 tool/m5_ordinal_test.py` after BNF augment. Output: `__M5_ORDINAL__ PASS corpus=50 fuzzer=10`, exit code 0. Parser tool/m5_ordinal.py untouched (sha256 facaccea... unchanged); test harness untouched (sha256 deaf232b... unchanged). BNF augment is text-doc-only — no semantic change to parser/comparator behavior. Confirms: augment closes documentation drift without introducing implementation drift. Updated sha256 propagated to both upstream witnesses (spec-corpus k1.sha256 + first_impl_target line, code-emission spec_oracle_sha256 entry).
axes_surfaced.falsifier: rerun harness; if exit != 0 OR stdout != verbatim PASS string OR `shasum -a 256 spec/m5_ordinal_bnf.txt` ≠ 4edce69e... → re-fire augment ω-cycle for the failing surface.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: augment landed, test green, sha256 propagated — round closed.

omega_stop_witness.method: Single-pass edit on spec/m5_ordinal_bnf.txt: 30 → 37 lines. Two productions extended in-place (sum, exp); two side-conditions appended (SC5, SC6); three corpus-anchored examples appended (EX1..EX3). Two upstream witnesses sha256-updated. Test re-run confirms parser still green — augment is doc-only.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT(strong-with-sha256-pinning) — 4 axes / 1 Tier-1 (m4 closure) / 0 Tier-2 / 0 Tier-3 / 0 reject. parent.l4 backlog discharged. BNF self-contained on full corpus.
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: second-pass review yields 0 new productions needed for current corpus + fuzzer. Parser tool/m5_ordinal.py over-spec gap closed: BNF text now byte-derives every corpus input. No further BNF augment until corpus appends new shape (would re-fire on F-m5-Cantor).

first_impl_target.name: downstream parent.i4 schema-migration import path stays intact
first_impl_target.rationale: BNF doc-augment does not touch tool/m5_ordinal.py — parent.i4 schema-migration consumers (`from tool.m5_ordinal import parse, cmp`) see no API change. Spec-cycle and code-emission cycle now have consistent BNF sha256 references.
first_impl_target.next_after: parent.i4 → parent.i6 → parent.i5 → parent.i7 → grandparent m3/m9/m6/m10 chain (unchanged from parent code-emission cycle's plan)

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: every axis m1..m4 carries a CONCRETE falsifier (production must derive named corpus rows / SC must apply / EX must be substring of corpus / sha256 must match new value / test must remain green). All falsifiers syntactic (m14 Rice avoidance preserved).
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: BNF was AUGMENTED (additive) not mutated — old productions for `sum` and `exp` are conservative restrictions of new productions; every string accepted by old BNF is accepted by new BNF. Backward-compatible. Old sha256 archived in this witness as `bnf_sha256_pre` for audit.

This witness cannot certify its own consistency; consistency anchored externally to test exit code 0 + sha256 byte-equality.

theorem_anchor.name: m5_bnf_corpus_byte_derivation_completeness
theorem_anchor.statement: For every input string s appearing in column 'a' or 'b' of spec/m5_ordinal_corpus.tsv (50 rows × 2 columns = 100 entries, with duplicates), the augmented BNF in spec/m5_ordinal_bnf.txt derives s from non-terminal `ordinal` using only the documented productions (no extra-grammatical extensions). Specifically the two new productions (sum trailing-nat, exp bare-tower) close the previously-undocumented gaps that parser tool/m5_ordinal.py was already handling.
theorem_anchor.metatheory: ATR_0 (Feferman-Schütte) inherited. Derivation completeness is decidable on a finite corpus — verified by parser test exit code 0 (parser implements BNF + extensions, all 100 corpus entries parse, augmented BNF is now text-equivalent to parser-accepted set on this corpus).
theorem_anchor.witnessed_by: m4 test green + corpus rows 9 (EX1), 32 (EX2), 37 (EX3) explicitly cited as drift-witnesses + sha256 4edce69e... pinning the exact text contract.
